mnemonics.title: LINEAR LINES
mnemonics.content: L - Linear direction (horizontal/vertical), I - Items in sequence, N - No overlapping, E - Even spacing (weight attribute), A - Arranged single-file, R - Relative positioning forbidden

mnemonics.title: APEX Layout
mnemonics.content: A - Absolute Positioning, P - Precise X/Y coordinates, E - Exact placement, X - XML requires manual calculations (deprecated)

mnemonics.title: FRAME STACK
mnemonics.content: F - Frame container, R - Rearrange views, A - All views overlap, M - Most recent on top, E - Empty placeholder

mnemonics.title: LAYOUT LAF
mnemonics.content: L - Linear (Ordered), A - Absolute (Precise), F - Frame (Layered) - Three fundamental layout approaches

acronyms.usage: Remember key attributes for LinearLayout: orientation=vertical/horizontal
Recall FrameLayout's primary use for single-view containers with overlays
Remember AbsoluteLayout uses exact X/Y coordinates (avoid in modern apps)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine books on a shelf - arranged strictly left-to-right or top-to-bottom
Picture a blueprint with components nailed to specific coordinates
Visualize photo layers in editing software - topmost layer is visible
See a balance scale - higher weight components occupy more space

analogies.analogy: Like train compartments - each car connected in strict sequence
Similar to furniture fixed with GPS coordinates in a room - doesn't adapt to room size changes
Like sticky notes on a bulletin board - last added note covers previous ones
Pizza slices - higher weight means bigger slice of screen space
